name: poetry
context: Manage Python project dependencies, virtual environments, builds, and publishing with Poetry using pyproject.toml and deterministic lock files
info: Poetry is Python's modern dependency management and packaging tool. It replaces the scattered workflow of pip, setuptools, and virtualenv with a single coherent tool. Poetry uses pyproject.toml for project metadata and dependency declarations, poetry.lock for deterministic reproducible installs, and manages isolated virtual environments automatically. Key workflows: scaffold projects with `poetry new` or `poetry init`, add/remove dependencies with `poetry add`/`poetry remove`, install from lock with `poetry install`, build sdist and wheel with `poetry build`, and publish to PyPI with `poetry publish`. Poetry supports dependency groups (dev, test, docs), version constraints (^, ~, >=), private registries, and environment variable configuration. For pip-based workflows, see [[python/pip]]. For the newer uv tool, see [[python/uv]].
body:
# Poetry — Python Dependency Management

Poetry is a single tool for dependency management, virtual environment handling, building, and publishing Python projects. It centers on `pyproject.toml` for declaration and `poetry.lock` for reproducibility.

## Project Setup

```bash
# Scaffold a new project with directory structure
poetry new my-project
# Creates:
#   my-project/
#     pyproject.toml
#     README.md
#     my_project/__init__.py
#     tests/__init__.py

# Initialize in an existing directory
cd existing-project
poetry init

# Use a specific Python interpreter
poetry env use python3.12

# Show environment details
poetry env info
```

## Dependency Management

```bash
# Add a runtime dependency
poetry add requests

# Add with version constraint
poetry add 'requests>=2.28,<3.0'

# Add a dev dependency
poetry add --group dev pytest

# Add to a custom group
poetry add --group docs sphinx

# Remove a dependency
poetry remove requests

# Install all dependencies from lock file
poetry install

# Install without dev dependencies (production)
poetry install --without dev

# Lock dependencies without installing
poetry lock

# Update all dependencies within constraints
poetry update

# Update a single package
poetry update requests

# Show dependency tree
poetry show --tree

# Export for pip compatibility
poetry export -f requirements.txt --output requirements.txt
```

## Running Code

```bash
# Run a command inside the poetry-managed venv
poetry run python script.py
poetry run pytest

# Activate the venv shell
poetry shell
```

## Building and Publishing

```bash
# Build sdist and wheel
poetry build

# Publish to PyPI
poetry publish

# Build and publish in one step
poetry publish --build

# Configure PyPI token
poetry config pypi-token.pypi pypi-XXXXXXXX
```

## pyproject.toml Structure

```toml
[tool.poetry]
name = "my-project"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "A short description"
authors = ["Your Name <you@example.com>"]
readme = "README.md"
packages = [{include = "my_project"}]

[tool.poetry.dependencies]
python = "^3.10"
requests = "^2.31"

[tool.poetry.group.dev.dependencies]
pytest = "^8.0"
mypy = "^1.8"

[tool.poetry.scripts]
my-cli = "my_project.cli:main"

[[tool.poetry.source]]
name = "private"
url = "https://private.pypi.org/simple/"
priority = "supplemental"

[build-system]
requires = ["poetry-core"]
build-backend = "poetry.core.masonry.api"
```

## Version Constraints

| Syntax | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `^1.2.3` | `>=1.2.3, <2.0.0` (caret — compatible releases) |
| `~1.2.3` | `>=1.2.3, <1.3.0` (tilde — patch-level) |
| `>=1.0,<2.0` | Explicit range |
| `*` | Any version |
| `==1.2.3` | Exact version |

## Configuration

Project-level `poetry.toml`:
```toml
[virtualenvs]
in-project = true
create = true
```

Environment variables:
- `POETRY_VIRTUALENVS_IN_PROJECT=true` — create `.venv` in project root
- `POETRY_VIRTUALENVS_CREATE=false` — skip venv creation (use system Python)
- `POETRY_PYPI_TOKEN_PYPI=pypi-xxx` — PyPI authentication token

sections.title: Project setup and initialization
sections.body: Use `poetry new my-project` to scaffold a new project with pyproject.toml, package directory, and tests directory. Use `poetry init` to add Poetry to an existing project interactively. Select a Python interpreter with `poetry env use python3.12`. Check environment details with `poetry env info`.

sections.title: Adding, removing, and locking dependencies
sections.body: Add packages with `poetry add <pkg>`. Use `--group dev` for dev dependencies, `--group <name>` for custom groups. Remove with `poetry remove <pkg>`. Install from lock with `poetry install`. Update within constraints with `poetry update`. View the dependency tree with `poetry show --tree`. Export for pip with `poetry export -f requirements.txt`.

sections.title: Building and publishing packages
sections.body: Run `poetry build` to produce sdist and wheel in the `dist/` directory. Publish to PyPI with `poetry publish` or `poetry publish --build` to combine both steps. Configure credentials with `poetry config pypi-token.pypi <token>` or the POETRY_PYPI_TOKEN_PYPI environment variable. For private registries, add a `[[tool.poetry.source]]` section in pyproject.toml.

sections.title: Configuration and environment variables
sections.body: Poetry reads configuration from poetry.toml (project-level) and the global config. Key settings: `virtualenvs.in-project = true` stores the venv as `.venv` in the project root. Environment variables follow the pattern POETRY_<SECTION>_<KEY>, e.g., POETRY_VIRTUALENVS_IN_PROJECT. Private registries are configured via `[[tool.poetry.source]]` with name, url, and priority fields.

scripts.name: Poetry Project Setup
scripts.description: Scaffold a new Poetry project, configure in-project venv, and add specified dependencies
scripts.inline:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

PROJECT_NAME="${PROJECT_NAME:-my-project}"
DEPS="${DEPENDENCIES:-}"

# Create new project
poetry new "$PROJECT_NAME"
cd "$PROJECT_NAME"

# Configure in-project virtualenv
poetry config virtualenvs.in-project true --local

# Add dependencies if specified
if [ -n "$DEPS" ]; then
  IFS=',' read -ra DEP_ARRAY <<< "$DEPS"
  for dep in "${DEP_ARRAY[@]}"; do
    dep=$(echo "$dep" | xargs)  # trim whitespace
    echo "Adding dependency: $dep"
    poetry add "$dep"
  done
fi

# Add common dev dependencies
poetry add --group dev pytest mypy

# Install everything
poetry install

# Report results
VENV_PATH=$(poetry env info --path)
echo ""
echo "Project created: $(pwd)"
echo "Virtualenv: $VENV_PATH"
echo "Run: cd $PROJECT_NAME && poetry run pytest"

PROJECT_NAME.description: Name for the new Poetry project

DEPENDENCIES.description: Comma-separated list of dependencies to add (e.g., 'requests,flask,sqlalchemy')

project_name.description: Name for the new Poetry project

dependencies.description: List of package dependencies to install (e.g., ['requests', 'flask>=3.0'])

project_path.description: Absolute path to the created project directory

venv_path.description: Absolute path to the Poetry-managed virtual environment

depends.reason: Poetry manages its own virtualenvs but builds on venv concepts

author: dojo-community
